questions.question: What is the key difference between an Observable and a Promise?
options: Observables are faster; Promises are more reliable
Observables are lazy and can emit multiple values over time; Promises are eager and resolve once
Promises can be cancelled; Observables cannot
Observables only work with HTTP calls; Promises work everywhere

questions.question: Why use switchMap instead of mergeMap for a search-as-you-type feature?
options: switchMap is faster because it uses Web Workers
mergeMap does not support HTTP calls
switchMap cancels the previous inner Observable when a new value arrives, preventing stale responses
switchMap retries failed requests automatically; mergeMap does not

questions.question: What does takeUntilDestroyed(destroyRef) do in an Angular component?
options: It deletes the component from the DOM when the Observable completes
It automatically completes the Observable and unsubscribes when the component is destroyed
It pauses the Observable stream until the component is re-initialised
It throws an error if the component is destroyed before the Observable emits

questions.question: A BehaviorSubject is initialised with 0. A new subscriber joins after the value has been set to 5. What value does the new subscriber receive immediately?
options: 0 — the initial value
Nothing — it only receives future emissions
5 — the current value replayed to new subscribers
An error, because the initial value was already consumed

questions.question: What does catchError(() => of([])) do inside a switchMap pipe?
options: It re-throws the error after logging it
It stops the outer Observable stream entirely
It swallows the error and replaces the failed inner Observable with one that emits an empty array
It retries the HTTP request up to three times before returning []
